style: Dry, high acid, medium/high tannin, sour cherry, herbal, earthy. Oak possible; food-friendly structure.

conclusions: Drink now to medium-term ageing (3-8 years)

common_student_errors: Confusing with Pinot Noir due to red fruit
Missing tannin or underestimating its level
Treating savoury and herbal notes as negative

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: High acidity red wines are underripe
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Sangiovese's high acidity is varietal and regional character, intentional and quality-supporting
Sangiovese's herb and earthy character is positive style expression and terroir identifier

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Italian wines are always low in tannin
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Sangiovese shows serious tannin structure comparable to other quality reds

coaching_hints: Link the high acidity and tannin to regional food-wine style.
Notice how the savoury character complements food.
Can you identify the sour cherry vs red plum distinction?
How does acidity contribute to drinkability with food?

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: High acidity and sour cherry fruit regardless of terroir
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Consistent acidity and cherry across Chianti bottlings

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Herbal, earthy, and savoury notes; good structure
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Oregano and thyme impressions; mineral finish

causal_reasoning_paths.cause: High acidity + medium+ tannin + food pairing philosophy
causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Wine designed to accompany Tuscan cuisine
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Pairs naturally with aged cheeses and cured meats
